title: BJP targets DMK leader Udhayanidhi Stalin over Sanatana Dharma remarks
author: The Hindu Bureau
content_html:
The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) on Tuesday (May 12, 2026) condemned DMK leader and former Tamil Nadu Deputy Chief Minister Udhayanidhi Stalin for his remarks on Sanatana Dharma in the State Assembly.

Through a post on X, Union Minister for Coal and Mines G. Kishan Reddy said: “DMK’s poisonous politics, anti-Hindu rhetoric, corruption and dynastic politics have all been vehemently rejected by the people of Tamil Nadu. This is not the first time Rahul Gandhi-led Congress INDI alliance leaders have spewed venom against Sanatana Dharma -- the civilisational soul of Bharat.”

“It is high time for your prejudiced party to introspect before the people completely eradicate your divisive and dangerous party,” he said.

BJP spokesperson Shehzad Poonawalla said, "This is not a coincidence but an exercise in vote-bank politics...earlier the Supreme Court had called this hate speech but Congress supported this statement. Congress, TMC, SP, and DMK all have one plan: insult Sanatan Hindus to run their vote-bank politics.”

He also targeted the Congress for “supporting” the anti-Hindu comment and questioned the “silence” of newly elected Chief Minister Vijay on the issue.

The party’s IT cell in charge, Amit Malviya, also said, “The last time Udhayanidhi Stalin spoke about ‘eradicating’ Sanatana Dharma, the courts called it hate speech and the people of Tamil Nadu pushed his party into the opposition. This time, he may be hiding behind the immunity that the legislature provides him, but there are some lines that cannot be crossed without consequence. Mocking and targeting the faith of millions is not political courage; it is arrogance. And this time, the divine retribution will be searing.”

Published - May 13, 2026 05:16 am IST

title: U.P. BJP announces State-wide protest over Samajwadi Party MP’s comment against PM Modi
author: Mayank Kumar
content_html:
The Bharatiya Janata Party’s (BJP), Uttar Pradesh unit on Tuesday (May 12, 2026) announced that they will organise protests at all the district headquarters in U.P. against the alleged objectionable comment made by Samajwadi Party (SP) Lok Sabha MP Ajendra Singh Lodhi on Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

“The BJP will organize massive protests at all the district headquarters of the state against the indecent and unbecoming remarks made by Samajwadi Party MP against the Honorable Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi. The protest will be organized at the district level in the Collectorate premises, the party’s women officials, public representatives and workers will participate along with a large number. The indecent language being used by SP MP on personal level under the guise of political opposition is against the healthy tradition of Indian politics. The BJP will not tolerate this and will oppose the SP,” State General Secretary of the BJP Sanjay Rai said. 

U.P. CM Yogi Adityanath described the comment as an insult to the trust of 145 crore countrymen and the democratic dignity of India. “The unparliamentary remarks made by an MP against the world’s most popular leader, respected Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi Ji are not only indecent and unforgivable, but are also a serious blow to Indian democratic norms. This act reveals political misbehavior, ideological bankruptcy and disrespect for the decency of public life. This is also an insult to the mandate, trust of 145 crore countrymen and the democratic dignity of India. The people of the country will definitely give a reply to such unruly behavior when the time comes,” wrote Mr. Adityanath on X.

Earlier, the SP Lok Sabha MP Ajendra Singh Lodhi was booked in Mahoba under charges of assault on a public official and promoting hate after he called Prime Minister Narendra Modi “anti-national,” police said on Tuesday (May 12, 2026). Mr. Lodhi, the Lok Sabha MP from the Hamirpur constituency, was booked on May 11, under sections 132 (assault on a public official), 189(2) (unlawful assembly), 196(1) (promoting enmity), and 352 (intentional insult) of the BNS. On May 11, Mr. Lodhi and his supporters submitted an 11-point memorandum to the Power department flagging power cuts and issues with smart metres, among other issues. The BJP alleged that the SP MP’s march to the government office caused a blockade, disrupted traffic, and hindered the work of public employees. He also alleged that Mr. Lodhi called Mr. Modi “anti-national”.

A video of the incident, which surfaced on social media, showed the lawmaker criticising Mr. Modi’s austerity appeal to the people regarding gold and fuel consumption. The SP MP allegedly used an expletive against Mr. Modi and called him a “desh-virodhi [anti-national]” Prime Minister.

The SP president Akhilesh Yadav distanced the party from the comment. “We have never been in favour of such language being used against anyone. I am not in favour of what Ajendra Singh said. He expressed his pain, but the language used to express it was not right,” said Mr. Yadav in Prayagraj. 

Published - May 13, 2026 07:44 am IST

  • Email

title: Dharmendra Pradhan’s mind in Odisha while his body in Delhi, BJD charges over NEET paper leak
author: Satyasundar Barik
content_html:
The Biju Janata Dal (BJD) on Tuesday (May 12, 2026) took a swipe at Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, alleging that recurring NEET paper leaks reflect a lack of focus, with “his mind in Odisha while his body is in Delhi.”

“Mr. Pradhan remains constantly engaged in party activities while neglecting the responsibilities of his ministry. Since the Union Education Minister’s mind remains in Odisha while his body is in Delhi, NEET examination paper leaks are happening repeatedly,” said Chinmaya Sahoo, President of BJD’s youth wing, addressing a press conference.

Alleging that under the BJP Government, examination cancellations due to paper leaks have become a routine occurrence, the regional party said BJP came to power promising the dream of “New India,” while it was creating an identity of “Leak India.”  The party has strongly criticised the Centre it was fourth cancellation within the last six years.

“If he [Mr. Pradhan] is unable to fulfil his ministerial responsibilities, he should immediately resign on moral grounds,” the party demanded.

The Central government played with the future of 22 lakh students as it is incapable of understanding the hard work and dedication of an examinees, Biju Chhatra Janata Dal Ipsita Sahu.

Taking pot shots at State’ Higher Education Minister Suraj Suryabanshi, she said, “just as the Union Education Minister is, similarly his ‘disciple’ in the Mr. Suryabanshi, is no different. Due to 23 paper leak incidents within the last 23 months in the State, the State Government is playing with the future of meritorious students.”

“While students stay awake through sleepless nights preparing for examinations and waiting for their results, BJP leaders who are playing with their fate are unable to understand their pain and suffering. Therefore, repeated incidents like paper leaks are being witnessed both in the State and across the country,” they pointed out.

Published - May 13, 2026 07:38 am IST

  • Copy link
  • Email
  • Facebook

title: FIR filed after videos show Bajrang Dal workers assaulting man over ‘love jihad’
author: Mehul Malpani
content_html:
The Madhya Pradesh Police on Tuesday (May 12, 2026) registered a case against various people, a day after videos showed a man being assaulted on allegations of ‘love jihad’ after being dragged out of a hotel in Bhopal, officials said. 

According to the police, a case was lodged at the Govindpura police station, based on various complaints received from individuals and community leaders, against unidentified persons for beating up Arif Khan (27) a resident of city’s Jahangirabad area.  

Bhopal Additional Commissioner of Police, Crime, Monika Shukla told The Hindu that the police have launched an investigation into the case and that the accused were yet to be identified.

On May 11, multiple videos went viral showing a group of Bajrang Dal activists barging into a private hotel’s room in Bhopal’s Gautam Nagar area, thrashing him and dragging him out. One video showed the mob, many of them donning saffron scarfs and chanting ‘Jai Shri Ram’, putting cow dung on his face and slapping him in front of the police even as personnel try to intervene, leading to arguments between the cops and Bajrang Dal workers. 

The man was later handed over to the police.

The right-wing activists had alleged that the man was caught with a Hindu woman and was “involved in an act of love jihad’, a term coined by the Hindu outfits accusing Muslim men of “luring” Hindu women into relationships with them. 

Govindpura station in-charge Awadhesh Singh Tomar said that the incident took place on May 10 and that a case has been registered under Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) Section 299 (Deliberate and malicious acts intended to outrage religious feelings of any class). 

Mr. Tomar said that the police are analysing the viral footage to identify the accused.

“We had taken a note of the matter yesterday [May 11, 2026] itself but the girl’s family and the man decided not to file any complaint. Now, we have received some complaints so an FIR has been filed,” he said, adding that the man and the woman have been given time to think in case they want to lodge a complaint. 

He said that Mr. Khan has at least five pending cases against him, including those of fraud and theft. 

Another police officer, who requested anonymity, said that while official statements of the woman were not recorded, she admitted to have been in a relationship with the man and had gone to the hotel consensually. 

Meanwhile, Deepak Bundele, a Bhopal based lawyer and social activist, has written to the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) seeking its intervention into the matter and directions for the authorities to prevent incidents of moral policing in future.

Terming the incident a violation of fundamental and human rights, Mr. Bundele wrote, “Notices should be issued to the Director General of Police (DGP), Madhya Pradesh, and the District Magistrate (DM), Bhopal, seeking a report on an impartial investigation. Based on video evidence, the accused persons should be identified, and punitive action against them must be ensured.”

Published - May 13, 2026 07:32 am IST

  • Copy link
  • Email
  • Facebook
  • Twitter

  • LinkedIn
